buckets.title: WARNING in rmap_walk_file
buckets.details: DEBUG_RWSEMS_WARN_ON triggered with sem->magic = 0x0 (init sets to &sem self-pointer). Trigger path: kswapd -> shrink_node -> shrink_folio_list -> unmap_poisoned_folio -> try_to_unmap -> rmap_walk_file -> i_mmap_trylock_read. Means i_mmap_rwsem of an inode was freed/zeroed while kswapd held a reference. Potential UAF on inode struct via memory-failure path. syzkaller could not reproduce.
buckets.kernelctf_eligibility: Possibly. Requires deterministic repro; syzkaller failed to find one. Worth submitting to syzbot mailing list for triage; without repro it cannot be confirmed as kernelCTF-eligible.

lockdep_warn_class.note: Same root cause — circular dep mmap_lock -> cpuctx_mutex -> pmus_lock -> cpu_hotplug_lock. Two sightings, syzkaller bucketed under different titles. NOT kernelCTF-eligible (lock-ordering bug, not memory corruption).

dos_class.note: Most are syzkaller-overload artifacts on this triple-sanitizer debug kernel (KASAN+SLUB_DEBUG_ON+LOCKDEP+UBSAN). Typically do not reproduce on production kernels. NOT kernelCTF-eligible.

interpretation: 1 potential memory-corruption finding (rwsem-magic-corruption in mm/memory-failure path) but with no deterministic reproducer. Pipeline end-to-end validated: kernel + KVM-passthrough syz-manager + CPU LLM + agent.loop with full bug-class classifier. The corpus-seed warm-start matters: v2 hit 143k PCs vs v1's 130k cold-start in the same hardware, and v2 surfaced 6 NEW bucket classes (lockdep-deadlock, rwsem-magic-corruption, iterate_supers hang, no-output, do_coredump hang) absent from v1.
